100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 1, 1926, the United States celebrated the continued economic growth of the Roaring Twenties, with stock market values reaching new heights. In Europe, tensions were palpable as political unrest stirred in Germany, where discussions of reparations from World War I remained unresolved. Meanwhile, in the realm of science, British physicist Ernest Rutherford announced significant advancements in nuclear research, hinting at the potential future of atomic energy.
